# Aurora Deep-Sea Research Station

The Aurora Deep-Sea Research Station is a fictional underwater laboratory
anchored to the Meridian Trench at a depth of exactly 2,847 meters. It was
commissioned in March 2019 by the (equally fictional) Thalassic Institute.

## Power system

Aurora draws its power from a closed-loop thorium micro-reactor nicknamed
"Lantern". Lantern produces 4.2 megawatts at peak output and is refueled
once every eleven years. A backup bank of vanadium flow batteries can carry
the station for 96 hours if Lantern goes offline.

## Crew and research

The station hosts a rotating crew of 14 researchers. Its flagship project,
codenamed GLASS EEL, studies bioluminescent signalling in deep-water
cephalopods. The lead scientist of GLASS EEL is Dr. Imogen Vasquez-Thorn,
who has held the post since 2021.

## Resupply

Resupply arrives by autonomous submarine every 40 days from the port of
Puerto Williams. The submarine, called "Pelican-9", carries at most
3.5 tonnes of cargo per run.
